title: Photon
description: Open-source geocoder for OpenStreetMap data with search-as-you-type and reverse geocoding, powered by an embedded OpenSearch index.
installation: Photon is a self-contained geocoder: it bundles its own OpenSearch/Elasticsearch search engine and serves an HTTP API on port 2322 (no external database add-on is needed). It requires a pre-built search index. The rtuszik/photon-docker image used here automatically downloads the GraphHopper index dump for the configured REGION on first start and keeps it updated. IMPORTANT: index dumps are very large (a country like 'germany' is several GB; the worldwide 'planet' index is ~90-200GB) and the initial download/extraction can take a long time, so size the /photon/data volume to match your chosen REGION (the 1Gi default below is only enough for a tiny region/testing - increase it before deploying a real region). Set REGION to a country/continent code (e.g. 'germany', 'europe') or 'planet' for worldwide. Query the API at /api?q=<text> and reverse-geocode at /reverse?lon=<x>&lat=<y>. No login or secrets to rotate. To pin a version instead of 'latest', use one of the rtuszik/photon-docker release tags.
